condition.qualitative_description: Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicates a drought episode sufficient to cause significant crop water stress and yield loss.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture deficit disrupting critical crop growth stages.

Latest kharif seasonal rainfall is below 75% of the long-term mean annual precipitation, and the multi-year annual precipitation trend is declining, confirming a structural rainfall deficit rather than a single anomalous year.
Farmers report well or borewell deepening over 5–10 years, or seasonal failure of shallow wells, indicating co-occurring groundwater depletion alongside surface irrigation failure.
Surface water body total area trend is below 1 ha/year and 15 or fewer distinct SWBs are present in the MWS, indicating shrinking storage infrastructure.
20 or fewer cumulative soil and water conservation or irrigation works recorded under MGNREGA for the MWS, indicating historically low investment in water harvesting infrastructure.
Mean cropping intensity at or below 1.15 with no upward trend over the observation period confirms persistent single-season rainfed farming with no expansion of irrigated double-cropping.

No canal irrigation infrastructure intersects the micro-watershed AND 35 or fewer MGNREGA irrigation works have been recorded, jointly confirming absence of both formal surface irrigation and community-level irrigation investment.

Many pending or rejected FRA claims with reported NTFP decline and forest conversion indicate tenure insecurity confirming encroachment vulnerability; high recognition weakens encroachment as primary driver.
More than 30 hectares of cumulative deforestation recorded in the micro-watershed, representing a significant absolute loss of forest habitat.

Farmers report moderate-to-high borewell/tubewell density (roughly 10–50 or more than 50 within 2–3 km) in hard rock terrain, indicating localised over-extraction not captured by block-level SOGE. Very low density or widespread borewell failure may indicate aquifer limits rather than density-driven stress.
Farmers report salty/brackish well water, white crust on soil or pipes, or water unsuitable for sensitive crops — indicating salinity from deep penetration or over-extraction.

Farmers report moderate-to-high borewell/tubewell density (roughly 10–50 or more than 50 within 2–3 km) in alluvium aquifers, indicating localised over-extraction not captured by block-level SOGE. Very low density or widespread borewell failure may indicate aquifer limits rather than density-driven stress.
Farmers report salty/brackish well water, white crust on soil or pipes, or water unsuitable for sensitive crops — indicating salinity from deep penetration or over-extraction.
The multi-year mean annual groundwater recharge (P - ET - Runoff) is negative, indicating chronic depletion rather than a single drought year anomaly.
Dominant aquifer class is crystalline basement with less than 20% alluvial lithology, confirming low recharge capacity and high vulnerability to depletion.
Farmers report salty/brackish well water, white crust on soil or pipes, or water unsuitable for sensitive crops — indicating salinity from deep penetration or over-extraction.
Farmers report borewell or dug-well deepening over recent years, or seasonal failure of shallow wells during rabi or zaid, indicating declining static water levels.

Farmers report moderate-to-high borewell/tubewell density (roughly 10–50 or more than 50 within 2–3 km) in hard rock terrain, indicating localised over-extraction not captured by block-level SOGE. Very low density or widespread borewell failure may indicate aquifer limits rather than density-driven stress.
Farmers report salty/brackish well water, white crust on soil or pipes, or water unsuitable for sensitive crops — indicating salinity from deep penetration or over-extraction.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicates sustained crop-season water deficit.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with less than 50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture deficit disrupting critical crop growth stages.

condition.expression: mean_kharif_precipitation < 0.75 * mean_annual_precipitation_mm or mean_annual_precipitation_mm < 700 or trend_annual_precipitation_mm < -5
condition.qualitative_description: Kharif seasonal rainfall below 75% of the long-term annual mean, or mean annual precipitation below 700 mm, or a declining trend exceeding 5 mm/year confirms structural precipitation deficit underpinning drought risk.

SPI at or below -1.0 (moderate meteorological drought), MAI below 0.5 (soil moisture inadequacy), and VCI below 35 (significant vegetation stress) together confirm concurrent meteorological and agricultural drought per India Drought Manual classification.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicates persistent crop-season water deficit sufficient to cause yield loss.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicates a drought episode sufficient to cause significant crop water stress and yield loss.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with less than 50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture deficit disrupting crop growth stages.

Kharif seasonal precipitation below 75% of the long-run kharif normal, or mean annual precipitation below 1000 mm with a declining trend exceeding 10 mm/year, confirms a rainfall deficit context consistent with recurrent drought.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicates persistent crop-season water deficit sufficient to cause yield loss.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture stress during critical crop growth stages.

Mean annual precipitation below 950 mm, or kharif seasonal precipitation below 700 mm in the latest year, or a declining trend exceeding 10 mm/year with mean below 1100 mm, indicates structural rainfall deficit in this sub-humid zone.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicates a drought episode sufficient to cause significant crop water stress and yield loss.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with less than 50% of historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture deficit disrupting critical crop growth stages.

Kharif seasonal rainfall below 75% of the long-term annual mean, or a declining multi-year precipitation trend below 900 mm/year, confirms meteorological drought as the primary driver of water scarcity.

Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicate persistent crop-season water deficit sufficient to cause yield loss in rice and other kharif crops.

Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with less than 50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged intra-seasonal moisture gaps that disrupt critical crop growth stages.

Kharif seasonal rainfall in the most recent year below 75% of the long-term annual mean, or mean annual precipitation below 1200 mm, indicates a below-normal rainfall year consistent with meteorological drought in AER-13.

Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicate a drought episode sufficient to cause significant crop water stress in an otherwise humid zone.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate a prolonged moisture deficit disrupting crop growth stages.

Monsoon onset is later than the earliest year on record AND kharif seasonal precipitation in the most recent year is below 80% of the multi-year mean, jointly indicating a delayed and deficient monsoon.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicate persistent crop-season water deficit sufficient to cause yield loss.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture stress during critical crop growth stages.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicate meaningful crop-season water deficit even in a perhumid zone.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture stress periods that disrupt crop growth stages.

Kharif seasonal precipitation below 75% of the long-run annual mean, or a declining precipitation trend exceeding 20mm/year combined with mean annual rainfall below 1800mm, signals structural drought risk in this perhumid zone.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicate significant meteorological drought stress even in a normally humid island ecoregion.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate a prolonged moisture deficit disrupting critical crop growth stages.

Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicating recurrent drought episodes sufficient to cause significant crop water stress and yield loss.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with less than 50% historical rainfall), indicating prolonged moisture deficit disrupting critical crop growth stages.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicate meaningful crop-season water deficit even in a normally humid zone.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate a prolonged intra-seasonal break that disrupts crop water supply.

Kharif seasonal precipitation in the latest year below 75% of the long-run kharif normal, or a declining trend in annual precipitation exceeding 20 mm/year, confirms a structural rainfall deficit underpinning drought.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicates persistent crop-season water deficit sufficient to cause yield loss.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged rainfall gaps that disrupt critical crop growth stages.

Long-run mean annual precipitation below 600 mm combined with a kharif season receiving less than 75% of the long-run annual mean confirms a below-normal monsoon year in an already moisture-limited zone.
Severe drought return period is 4 years or less, or moderate drought return period is 4 years or less, indicate meaningful crop-season water deficit even in a perhumid zone.
Mean dry-spell weeks across the record is 3 or more (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall), indicate prolonged moisture stress capable of disrupting crop growth stages.
More than 75% of cropped area is under single kharif season only, indicating near-total dependence on monsoon rainfall with negligible irrigation-supported rabi or double cropping.

No irrigation canal intersects the micro-watershed and 35 or fewer MGNREGA irrigation works have been constructed, confirming absence of both surface irrigation infrastructure and public investment in water access.
No canal irrigation infrastructure intersects the micro-watershed, and 35 or fewer MGNREGA irrigation works have been recorded, confirming absence of both surface and publicly-invested irrigation.
More than 75% of cropped area is under single kharif season only, indicating near-total dependence on monsoon rainfall with negligible irrigation-supported double cropping.

No irrigation canal intersects the micro-watershed and 35 or fewer MGNREGA irrigation works have been constructed, confirming absence of both formal surface irrigation and community-level irrigation investment.

Surface water body total area trend is below 1 ha/year and 15 or fewer distinct SWBs are present in the MWS, indicating shrinking storage infrastructure.
20 or fewer cumulative soil and water conservation or irrigation works recorded under MGNREGA for the MWS, indicating historically low public investment in water harvesting infrastructure.

Farmers report that well or borewell depth has increased noticeably over recent years, or that pumping costs have risen substantially, suggesting declining groundwater levels that increase the cost and difficulty of lift irrigation — particularly for marginal farmers.

Surface water body area is declining at more than 5 ha/year, or the latest year area is below 85% of the multi-year mean, indicating siltation, structural failure, or reduced catchment inflows.
20 or fewer cumulative soil and water conservation or irrigation works recorded under MGNREGA for the micro-watershed, indicating historically low investment in water harvesting infrastructure.

Fewer than 3 surface water bodies in the MWS combined with cooperative distance exceeding 15 km, indicating both a structural infrastructure gap and limited access to institutional credit for irrigation investment.
Surface water body total area trend is below 1 ha/year and 15 or fewer distinct SWBs are present in the MWS, indicating contraction of storage infrastructure.
20 or fewer cumulative soil and water conservation or irrigation works recorded under MGNREGA for the MWS, indicating historically low investment in water harvesting infrastructure.

Farmers report that shallow dug wells or hand pumps used for supplemental irrigation have dried earlier in the season compared to previous years, or that pump operating costs have increased due to greater lift depth. This is not directly measurable from available data but is a key field indicator of groundwater supplemental irrigation failure in alluvial settings.
Surface water body total area trend is below 1 ha/year and 15 or fewer distinct SWBs are present in the MWS, indicating shrinking storage infrastructure relative to irrigable area.

20 or fewer cumulative soil and water conservation or irrigation works recorded under MGNREGA, indicating historically low investment in water harvesting infrastructure relative to the irrigation needs of a hilly MWS.

Farmers report that open wells or shallow borewells run dry or yield insufficient water during the pre-monsoon months (March–May) or during extended dry spells within the kharif season, indicating that groundwater backup for irrigation is also unreliable.

Declining surface water body area (more than 2 ha/year loss) or very few small water bodies relative to the micro-watershed, indicating insufficient or shrinking storage infrastructure on these small island territories.
20 or fewer cumulative soil and water conservation or irrigation works recorded under MGNREGA for the micro-watershed, indicating historically low investment in water harvesting infrastructure.
Cumulative deforestation exceeds 20 ha and afforestation covers less than half the deforested area, indicating a net deficit in forest recovery.

Distance to nearest APMC market exceeding 30 km indicates that small holders face high transaction costs and are likely dependent on intermediaries for price discovery, reducing net farm income.
